findings.description: SAMHSA logo appears in a slightly different position and style in HTML compared to PDF
findings.evidence: PDF shows the SAMHSA logo in the upper right corner with specific styling; HTML shows it top-left with slightly different rendering

findings.description: Cover page layout differs - PDF has centered title within a bordered box, HTML renders it without the border box
findings.evidence: PDF shows content within a dark blue bordered rectangle; HTML shows content without this decorative border

findings.description: Title color styling differs slightly between PDF and HTML
findings.evidence: PDF title 'Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration (SAMHSA) Unified Performance Reporting Tool (SUPRT) - C' appears in dark teal/black; HTML renders similarly but with slightly different weight/styling

findings.description: The consent section box border styling differs
findings.evidence: PDF shows CLIENT CONSENT section within a bordered box with OMB info at top; HTML shows it without the box border as a flowing page

findings.description: Public reporting burden notice box styling differs
findings.evidence: PDF shows the public reporting burden text in a bordered box at bottom of page; HTML shows it in a light gray rounded box

findings.description: Section A header styling differs - PDF has light blue shaded header bar, HTML has light gray shaded header bar
findings.evidence: PDF: 'A. SOCIAL DRIVERS OF HEALTH' in a light blue shaded bar; HTML: same text in a light gray shaded bar

findings.description: Question numbering format slightly differs in indentation and alignment
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'A1.' with specific left-aligned indentation; HTML shows 'A1.' with slightly different spacing but same content structure

findings.description: Office use only section box border styling differs
findings.evidence: PDF has the record management section in a bordered box; HTML displays without the border box but content is intact

findings.description: Nested <main> elements in the HTML. The document has two nested <main> tags, which is semantically incorrect but does not affect content fidelity.
The OMB number and expiration date appear multiple times in the HTML, mirroring the PDF's repeated header/footer pattern. This is acceptable as it reflects the source document's structure.
findings.evidence: OMB No. 0930-0400 / Expires: 01/31/2028 appears on the cover page rendering and again in the consent section, matching the PDF.
